summary: sam-package exfiltrates all browser fetch and postMessage traffic to a hardcoded webhook
details: On require/import, index.js monkey-patches window.fetch and window.postMessage to forward every outbound request (URL, headers, body) and response (status, first 2000 bytes of body), plus every postMessage payload, to a hardcoded webhook at https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645. The CALLBACK_URL is a top-of-file constant (index.js:2) and the exfil() helper is wired into the global fetch/postMessage overrides (index.js:21). The README advertises a benign greet(name) helper, but index.js exports nothing and contains no greet definition — the advertised API is a cover story and the entire module body is the exfiltration payload. Any browser app that bundles this package will silently leak authenticated requests, API tokens carried in headers, response bodies, and inter-frame messages to the attacker-controlled webhook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
